differential_diagnoses: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system
next_steps: Send CSF for autoimmune encephalitis panel (including LGI1, CASPR2, AMPAR, GABA-B) and paraneoplastic panel
Send serum for autoimmune encephalitis panel and paraneoplastic antibodies
Perform whole-body PET/CT scan to screen for occult malignancy
Initiate empiric high-dose intravenous methylprednisolone for suspected autoimmune/inflammatory process
Perform repeat lumbar puncture if initial autoimmune panels are non-diagnostic, including cytology and flow cytometry for lymphoma
Consider brain biopsy if non-invasive workup is inconclusive
Continue levetiracetam or other antiepileptic therapy due to EEG findings

differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune encephalitis (e.g., LGI1 or GABA-B receptor antibody-associated)
Primary angiitis of the central nervous system
next_steps: Perform CSF analysis including cytology, flow cytometry, paraneoplastic and autoimmune encephalitis antibody panels, and RT-QuIC for CJD
Initiate empiric high-dose intravenous corticosteroids for suspected inflammatory/autoimmune process
Conduct whole-body PET/CT scan to screen for occult malignancy
Initiate levetiracetam for seizure control given epileptiform EEG findings
Perform high-resolution MRI of the brain with and without gadolinium contrast and post-steroid follow-up imaging
Consider brain biopsy if initial autoimmune and malignancy workup is inconclusive
